comments.post_body:
I’m happy to share that I’m starting a new position as Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft!

This milestone holds deep meaning, shaped not only by hard work but also by profound personal experiences. Becoming a mother and navigating a significant health journey have been humbling chapters that strengthened my resilience and reshaped my perspective. These experiences showed me the power of community and the importance of gratitude.

I am deeply thankful to my family, mentors, and colleagues whose unwavering support made this possible. As I embrace this new role, I look forward to contributing with renewed purpose, empathy, and appreciation for the journey that brings us forward together.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their new position as Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft, responding to their post about the personal and professional journey that led to this milestone. The author's story highlights the importance of resilience, community, and gratitude in achieving their goals.

comments.post_body:
This week I said goodbye to a people that made work feel like home.
Not the building, not the badge — the people.
The ones who sat beside me through deadlines, laughed with me on the hard days, and clapped louder for my wins than I did for myself.

There’s a picture of them holding a sign saying “Go crush it” — and it’s more than a send-off.
It’s love wrapped as confidence, and I’m taking that energy with me as I step into something new. I feel overwhelmed with the love I received from everyone at OneSpan  :)

And now THE BIG NEWS.
I’m thrilled to be joining Microsoft, and diving into a whole new world of cool problems, brilliant people, and probably 14 different calendar links for the same meeting.😝
But hey,  new role, new challenges, same caffeine dependency.

To the Onespan's amazing people and my team — thank you for cheering me louder than my alarm clock in the morning. Kinga Nowicka  Pratik Dhanadhya Sajeewa Andrado Yen Liu Yin Lin William La Berge Ahana Dwivedi Kanwar Singh

And to my old friend and new teammate, a special thanks since he made this new beginning possible with all his guidance and support Adit Sheth 😊
To what’s next — let’s go. 💙🚀
comments.context_summary: The commenter is bidding farewell to their old job at OneSpan, thanking their colleagues for their support and love, and announcing their new role at Microsoft, expressing excitement for the new challenges and opportunities ahead. They're grateful for the guidance and support that made this new beginning possible.

comments.comment_body: Hope you and your family are doing fine
comments.post_body:
Covid-19 and Air Travel

The airlines mandate a RT-PCR test before boarding a flight. The test can be done at most 72 hours prior to flight.

This mandate does not rule out the possibility of Covid transmission completely.

I got Covid positive while coming from New Delhi to San Francisco.  I am not 100% sure from where did I get it. But my Covid Test on second day after arrival was negative. 

My symptoms appeared after four days and second Covid test on 5th day after arrival came positive. Unfortunately, the whole family was affected by that time. 

I did not see any news about my flight, but a quick web search showed similar incidents:

https://lnkd.in/gMfTEPP

https://lnkd.in/g2aGiBT

My learnings:
- Isolate and quarantine for at least four days after travel
- Do not rush for a Covid test without symptoms
- PPE kit in the flight may decrease your chance for infection

Stay safe!
comments.context_summary: The commenter shares their personal experience of contracting Covid-19 while traveling from New Delhi to San Francisco, despite testing negative initially, and highlights the importance of isolation, quarantine, and precautions like PPE kits to minimize the risk of transmission. They respond to the post about Covid-19 and air travel, discussing the limitations of pre-flight RT-PCR tests in preventing Covid transmission.

comments.post_body:
I'm excited to share that I have successfully defended my Ph.D. Thesis on "Essays on Information Design for Online Retailers and Social Networks". This journey was extremely fulfilling and was possible due to the guidance of my advisors Prof. Ramandeep Randhawa and Prof. Kimon Drakopoulos. Thank you to my committee members, Prof. Phebe Vayanos and Prof. Andrew Daw, for their invaluable feedback. I’m also thankful to the USC Marshall’s Data Sciences and Operations (DSO) family for their support.

Lastly, I’m grateful to my parents, family members, and friends for being with me through all times. Your belief in me has made this day possible!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post announcing the successful defense of a Ph.D. thesis on information design for online retailers and social networks, and is likely to offer congratulations and praise. The original post expresses gratitude to advisors, committee members, and loved ones for their support throughout the journey.

May end marked an end to my journey at Cognex, I was among the people Cognex had to let go due to the difficulties caused by the pandemic #Covid19. While at Cognex I got the opportunity to work on really diverse problems and roles. I shall always remember fondly the time I spent at Cognex, along with the wonderful colleagues and leaders I met along the way, some of whom became friends I shall forever cherish!
That being said, I am looking forward to next challenge ahead, and am looking to explore new opportunities in the areas of Computer Vision/Image Processing and Deep Learning. If you or anyone you know is hiring in this space, it would be great to connect! #layoffs #covid19impact
comments.context_summary: The commenter is sharing their experience of being laid off from Cognex due to pandemic difficulties and is now looking for new opportunities in Computer Vision, Image Processing, and Deep Learning. They are open to connecting with potential employers or recruiters in this space.

After 4 awesome years, my Uber trip is coming to an end.  I will be departing from Uber today.

I want to thank everyone I've worked with.  Recruiters, sourcers, engineers, managers, coordinators, candidates, and anyone else who contributed to my experience and growth.  The people I've spent time with over the last 4 years have made it all worth it.

Uber has been a wild and unforgettable ride.  I'm excited to watch this company evolve and continue to impact the world in years to come.

My new chapter starts on Monday... stay tuned!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is bidding farewell to their colleagues at Uber after 4 years, expressing gratitude for the experience and people they've worked with, and is excited for their new chapter starting soon. comments.post_body:
I wrote my first computer program sometime back in year 2011. Fascinated I decided to pursue a career path that would let me program to my heart's content. The environment in my undergrad, #BITSPilani, was conducive of my ambition, and I couldn’t be more grateful. Long story short almost eight years later tomorrow I’m going to start  as a Software Developer at #Microsoft, Redmond with emphasis on #BigData & #DataEngineering in their Business Application Organization. 

In this story I’ve rounded off the edges, cut out my lucky breaks and left out the days of self-doubt. It hasn’t been a smooth journey. Then, I didn’t expect it to be either - there is no triumph without toll.  I’m thankful for the struggles I experienced, and don’t regret any of my decisions. My family and friends have been a constant source of support. I cannot emphasize enough the role they’ve played to help me achieve this milestone.

#BITSian #Microsoft #Job #Redmond #LearnerForLife
comments.context_summary: The commenter is sharing their personal story of becoming a software developer, from writing their first program in 2011 to landing a job at Microsoft, and expressing gratitude for the support of their family and friends throughout their journey. They are reflecting on the challenges and triumphs of their path to achieving this milestone.

comments.post_body:
Are you a Data Scientist looking for a Job? Are you a Recruiter looking for a Data Scientist? If you answered yes or NO to this questions you need to read this.

I created a blog post with a lot of information about the two sides of getting a job as a data scientist, the data scientist side and also the recruiter side. 

Here it is:

https://lnkd.in/ejSdpTK

This is a post I authored but it contains a lot of information I got from my connections. Mostly from Amanda Voss. I will be creating a webinar soon that will follow this blog post, so be ready for that :).

I the post you'll find great advice from a lot of great Data Scientist like Kyle McKiou, Vin Vashishta, Eric Weber, Mark Meloon, Matthew Mayo, Beau Walker, JT Kostman, PhD and Brandon Rohrer. And also amazing advice and blog posts and articles I found online.

I hope you like it!

Have fun!
comments.context_summary: The author is sharing a blog post they created, which provides information and advice for both data scientists looking for a job and recruiters looking to hire them, and announces an upcoming webinar on the topic. The blog post features insights from various data science professionals and other online resources.

comments.post_body: When you are not using the free desktop version of Deep Cognition, Inc. with Project Jupyter’s JupiyterLab to train tour models on your cluster.

comments.post_body:
Want to work in data science at LinkedIn? Know a talented friend or colleague who does? I want to hear from you! Desired characteristics:

1. 2-3 years of work experience with a focus on data. Not necessarily just in a data science position.

2. Intermediate to expert level in SQL and R/Python.

3. Strong understanding of and experience on projects using machine learning and statistical modeling.

4. Great communicator. Can tell a story with data.

5. Dreams big. Doesn't accept the status quo.

Keep in mind that when I post something like this I get a ton of messages, so I can't respond to each one. However, I will read them.

Reaching out to me is completely confidential. It stays between you and I unless you want to move forward in the process.

Tag people you think would be a good fit in the comments!

Come join us: Dream big, have fun and get s*** done.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about potential job opportunities in data science at LinkedIn, where the author is looking for referrals for talented individuals with experience in data, machine learning, and statistical modeling. The commenter is likely reaching out to express interest or recommend someone for the role.

comments.post_body:
How to get started with Deep Learning for Data Science? 

So, this is one of the posts I was mentioning earlier. Deep Learning is so important right now. It's not just adding layers to a neural network, is a complex and interesting field with lots of space for research and development. 

It's not easy to get started but I'll try my best to guide you through this process. Check out this resources, but remember, this is not only watching videos and reading papers, it's about understanding, programming, coding, failing and then making it happen. 

-1. Learn Python and R ;)

0. Andrew Ng and Coursera (you know, he doesn't need an intro): 

- https://lnkd.in/eUe9YZE

1. Siraj Raval: He's amazing. He has the power to explain hard concepts in a fun and easy way. Follow him on his YouTube channel. Specifically this playlists:

- The Math of Intelligence: https://lnkd.in/eYPJbsW

- Intro to Deep Learning: https://lnkd.in/e4Sg9qy

2. François Chollet's book: Deep Learning with Python (and R soon):

- https://lnkd.in/gfV2ery
- https://lnkd.in/e6_YGqx

3. IBM Cognitive Class: 

- https://lnkd.in/eNKPSnJ
- https://lnkd.in/eBVRf-R

4. Medium blogs:

- https://lnkd.in/eaUx5aN
- https://lnkd.in/eGaQwts

5. DataCamp:

- https://lnkd.in/eWVz7e5
- https://lnkd.in/ezXBq6M

6. Have fun :)
comments.context_summary: The commenter is providing guidance on getting started with deep learning for data science, sharing various resources such as online courses, books, and YouTube channels to help beginners learn and understand the complex field. The commenter emphasizes that learning deep learning requires hands-on practice, programming, and perseverance, not just watching videos or reading papers.

comments.comment_body: Hi, I am interested. Kindly review my profile.

comments.post_body:
I will be presenting at #NIPS  Competition Track 2017, Long Beach, CA 8-9 Dec and let’s meet there if you are around ! Our team participated in a Machine Learning competition hosted by MSKCC (Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, world's no. 1 cancer hospital) on Kaggle called 
"Personalized Medicine: Redefining Cancer Treatment". It was about classifying clinically actionable genetic mutations with AI. Understanding the genetic mutations that really matter in a cancer tumor is a really challenging task with a potential huge impact on millions of lives. Not only our solution was selected as one of the most innovative solutions and has a potential to advance research in this field, we will also be authoring a Book Chapter in upcoming "Springer Series on Challenges in Machine Learning: NIPS 2017" with the details of our approach.
comments.context_summary: The author will be presenting at NIPS 2017 after participating in a Kaggle competition on personalized medicine, where their team developed an innovative AI solution for classifying genetic mutations in cancer tumors. They will also be authoring a book chapter on their approach in an upcoming Springer series.

comments.post_body:
I will be presenting at #NIPS  Competition Track 2017, Long Beach, CA 8-9 Dec and let’s meet there if you are around ! Our team participated in a Machine Learning competition hosted by MSKCC (Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, world's no. 1 cancer hospital) on Kaggle called 
"Personalized Medicine: Redefining Cancer Treatment". It was about classifying clinically actionable genetic mutations with AI. Understanding the genetic mutations that really matter in a cancer tumor is a really challenging task with a potential huge impact on millions of lives. Not only our solution was selected as one of the most innovative solutions and has a potential to advance research in this field, we will also be authoring a Book Chapter in upcoming "Springer Series on Challenges in Machine Learning: NIPS 2017" with the details of our approach.
